justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated (escalation #1), clock-start ambiguity identified (assumptions section notes '2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time' and edge case covers driver picking up at minute 14), wrong-address exception from Finance noted (BR-6), auto-refund vs. 3-day processing conflict noted (escalation #2), 9 rules extracted with correct invariant classifications, 5 formal escalations, and confidence.level = 38 (well under 50).

observations.text: PM/Legal contradiction is the lead escalation with detailed options and a pragmatic hybrid recommendation
Auto-refund vs. 3-business-day processing contradiction explicitly escalated as a separate item with clear resolution options
9 business rules extracted — exceeds the 5 minimum — with well-reasoned invariant classifications (e.g., driver rating correctly marked as non-invariant since threshold is tunable)
Clock-start ambiguity surfaced in assumptions and edge cases (driver picks up at minute 14, delivery at 2h05m)
Wrong-address exception (BR-6) captured and further escalated as needing precise definition
Confidence at 38 appropriately reflects the severity of unresolved contradictions
Rejection reasons clearly tie back to specific contradictions, explaining why acceptance criteria cannot be written
Edge cases are excellent — covers boundary conditions (exactly 10km), cascading failures (second driver cancellation), and geodesic vs. driving distance
Ubiquitous language includes 'Goodwill Refund' term that captures the Legal framing, showing the contradiction is embedded in the domain model itself
Business impact analysis correctly flags regulatory risk of guarantee language as HIGH

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'contradiction', got values: 'ambiguity', 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
